title: An uploaded CSV file can have any number of columns. You need to map those colum…

messages.content:
An uploaded CSV file can have any number of columns. You need to map those columns to the target data column as per your intelligence. 
for example 
serviceName can be Name or Service Name,
durationMinutes can Minutes or ServiceTime or duration.
So you need to figure out what column need to be mapped with correct target data points.
Please update Skills and Agent instruction as per given suggestion

I'll start by loading the agent context and the relevant skills simultaneously.

I've loaded the agent and skill. Now let me design and prepare the full updated content for both the **SKILL.md** and **Agent Instructions** with intelligent column mapping logic.

---

## 📋 Pending Changes Summary

Here is a full breakdown of **all changes** before I save anything. Please review and confirm.

---

### 🔷 Change 1 — Updated `partoplay-services-importer/SKILL.md`

A new **Step 1.5 — Intelligent Column Mapping** section will be inserted between Step 1 (Parse CSV) and Step 2 (Map to Schema). This teaches the agent to handle any CSV header variations by resolving them to known target fields using alias matching.

**Full updated SKILL.md:**

````markdown
---
name: partoplay-services-importer
description: >-
  Parses an uploaded Partoplay Services CSV file, maps every row to the target Services JSON schema,
  and saves each record individually to Cosmos DB using cosmos_create_document. Use this skill
  whenever a user uploads a CSV with service data for Partoplay, mentions importing services,
  mapping service records, or saving services to Cosmos DB with partitionKey 'Services'.
---

# Partoplay Services Importer

You are processing a Partoplay Services CSV upload. Follow every step below in strict order. Do not skip any step.

## Step 1 — Parse the CSV

Read the uploaded CSV file in full. Extract the raw header row exactly as it appears — do not assume headers match any specific naming convention.

Parse every row into a structured list keyed by the raw column headers.

---

## Step 1.5 — Intelligent Column Mapping

The uploaded CSV may use any column naming convention. Before mapping rows to the target schema, resolve each raw CSV header to its target field using the alias table below. Matching is **case-insensitive** and **whitespace-insensitive**.

| Target Field | Accepted CSV Column Names (aliases) |
|---|---|
| `serviceCode` | ServiceCode, Service Code, Code, Svc Code, SvcCode |
| `serviceName` | ServiceName, Service Name, Name, Svc Name, SvcName, Service Title, Title |
| `serviceCategory` | Category, ServiceCategory, Service Category, Cat, Svc Category |
| `serviceSubCategory` | Sub Category, SubCategory, Sub-Category, ServiceSubCategory, Sub Cat, SubCat |
| `businessUnitName` | BusinessUnitName, Business Unit, Business Unit Name, BU Name, BUName *(ignored — not mapped to schema)* |
| `serviceKind` | ServiceType, Service Type, Type, Kind, ServiceKind, Svc Type |
| `onlineBookingEnabled` | OnlineBooking, Online Booking, Online Book, Booking, IsOnline, OnlineEnabled |
| `taxIncluded` | TaxIncluded, Tax Included, Tax Inc, IncludesTax, IsTaxIncluded |
| `taxGroup` | TaxGroup, Tax Group, Tax Grp, TaxGrp, TaxCategory |
| `durationMinutes` | ServiceLength, Service Length, Duration, DurationMinutes, Minutes, ServiceTime, Length, Time, Mins |

**Mapping rules:**
1. For each raw CSV header, find the best match in the alias table (case-insensitive, ignore extra spaces).
2. If a header matches an alias, map all values in that column to the corresponding target field.
3. If a header has **no match** in the alias table, ignore that column entirely.
4. If a target field has **no matching column** in the CSV, apply its default value as defined in Step 2.
5. If two or more CSV columns resolve to the same target field, use the first one encountered and ignore the rest.
6. Before proceeding to Step 2, log the resolved mapping as: `CSV column "<raw>" → target field "<target>"` for every matched column.

---

## Step 2 — Map Each Row to the Target JSON Schema

Using the resolved column mapping from Step 1.5, produce a complete JSON document for **every** row:

| Target Field | Source | Rule |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | Auto-generated | Generate a new UUID v4 |
| `partitionKey` | Fixed | Always `"Services"` |
| `businessId` | Not in CSV | Always `""` (empty string) |
| `serviceId` | Same as `id` | Copy the same generated UUID used for `id` |
| `serviceCode` | Mapped column | Direct copy |
| `serviceName` | Mapped column | Direct copy |
| `serviceKind` | Mapped column | Direct copy |
| `serviceCategory` | Mapped column | Direct copy |
| `serviceSubCategory` | Mapped column | Direct copy |
| `isAddOn` | Not in CSV | Always `false` |
| `durationMinutes` | Mapped column | Parse as integer. If empty or missing → `0` |
| `price` | Not in CSV | Always `0` |
| `priceSource` | Not in CSV | Always `""` |
| `taxIncluded` | Mapped column | If empty/missing/blank → `null`. If `"true"`/`"1"`/`"yes"` → `true`. Else → `false` |
| `taxGroup` | Mapped column | If empty/missing/blank → `null`. Else direct copy as string |
| `onlineBookingEnabled` | Mapped column | `"true"`/`"1"`/`"yes"` → `true`. All else → `false` |
| `requiresResource` | Not in CSV | Always `""` |
| `requiresProvider` | Not in CSV | Always `""` |
| `resourceTypeRequired` | Not in CSV | Always `""` |
| `providerTypeRequired` | Not in CSV | Always `""` |
| `isActive` | Not in CSV | Always `true` |
| `createdAt` | Auto-generated | Current UTC timestamp in ISO 8601 format |
| `updatedAt` | Auto-generated | Same value as `createdAt` |
| `_etag` | Not in CSV | Always `null` |
| `_ts` | Not in CSV | Always `null` |

---

## Step 3 — Validate Before Saving

Before saving, verify each record:
- `id` is a non-empty UUID
- `partitionKey` is exactly `"Services"`
- `serviceCode` is non-empty
- `serviceName` is non-empty

If a record fails validation, log it as failed and skip saving it. Continue processing remaining rows.

---

## Step 4 — Save Each Record to Cosmos DB ⚠️ CRITICAL — DO NOT SKIP

For **every** validated record, invoke the tool `cosmos_create_document` with:
- `partitionKey` → `"Services"`
- Document body → the complete record JSON (pure JSON only — no markdown, no code block wrapping)

**Rules:**
- Process ALL rows. Do not stop after the first record.
- Do not batch — each row must be a separate `cosmos_create_document` call.
- If a save fails, record the error (serviceCode + error message) and continue to the next row.

---

## Step 5 — Report Results

After all records have been processed, provide a summary:

- ✅ **Total records successfully saved** (count)
- ❌ **Failed records** (list each with `serviceCode` and error reason)
- 📋 **Summary table** showing `serviceCode`, `serviceName`, and `status` for every row
````

---

### 🔷 Change 2 — Updated Agent Instructions

The agent instructions will have **Step 1** updated and a new **Step 1.5** (Intelligent Column Mapping) inserted, keeping all other steps and content exactly intact.

**Changes to agent instructions:**

- **Step 1** — Remove the hardcoded header list; instead say "extract raw headers as-is"
- **Step 1.5 (NEW)** — Full alias table and fuzzy-matching rules (same as SKILL.md above)
- **Step 2** — Reference "mapped column" instead of specific hardcoded CSV column names
- All other steps (3, 4, 5) remain **unchanged**

---

### ✅ Summary of All Changes

| Area | What Changes |
|---|---|
| `SKILL.md` — Step 1 | Parse raw headers without assuming names |
| `SKILL.md` — Step 1.5 *(new)* | Alias table + fuzzy mapping rules for all 9 target fields |
| `SKILL.md` — Step 2 | Source references updated to "Mapped column" |
| Agent Instructions — Step 1 | Parse raw headers without assuming names |
| Agent Instructions — Step 1.5 *(new)* | Same alias table + mapping rules |
| Agent Instructions — Step 2 | Source references updated to "Mapped column" |

No skills are added or removed. No other instruction sections are touched.

---

**👉 Please confirm — shall I go ahead and save these changes?**

Let me load the agent context and the skill details simultaneously to get the exact current content before saving.I need the correct agent ID. Let me ask the user — but first, I realize the agent ID wasn't passed. Let me try loading it properly. Could you please provide the **Agent ID** so I can load and save the correct agent? It should be visible in the agent editor URL or settings.

Alternatively, if you can share the **Agent ID**, I'll proceed immediately to save all the confirmed changes.

Let me load the agent context and the skill details simultaneously.I now have the full current content of both the agent and the skill file. Let me save both simultaneously — the updated SKILL.md and the updated agent instructions.All changes have been saved successfully! ✅ Here's a complete summary of what was updated:

---

## ✅ Changes Saved — Services Data Extracter Agent

### 📄 SKILL.md (`partoplay-services-importer`)

| Section | Change |
|---|---|
| **Step 1 — Parse the CSV** | Removed hardcoded header list. Now reads raw headers **exactly as they appear** without assumptions |
| **Step 1.5 — Intelligent Column Mapping** *(NEW)* | Full alias table with 10 target fields and all accepted variations |
| **Step 2 — Map Each Row** | Source column references updated to **"Mapped column"** (resolved dynamically from Step 1.5) |

### 🤖 Agent Instructions

| Section | Change |
|---|---|
| **Step 1 — Receive the CSV** | Removed hardcoded header list. Now extracts raw headers as-is |
| **Step 1.5 — Intelligent Column Mapping** *(NEW)* | Same alias table + 6 mapping rules inserted before Step 2 |
| **Step 2 — Map Each Row** | Source references updated to **"Mapped column"** |
| **Steps 3, 4, 5** | Completely **unchanged** |

---

### 🧠 How the Intelligent Mapping Works

The agent will now automatically resolve any CSV column to the correct target field:

| Target Field | Example CSV Columns Accepted |
|---|---|
| `serviceName` | `Name`, `Service Name`, `SvcName`, `Service Title`, `Title` |
| `durationMinutes` | `Minutes`, `ServiceTime`, `Duration`, `Mins`, `Length`, `Time` |
| `serviceCode` | `Code`, `Svc Code`, `ServiceCode`, `SvcCode` |
| `serviceKind` | `Type`, `ServiceType`, `Kind`, `Svc Type` |
| `onlineBookingEnabled` | `OnlineBooking`, `Booking`, `IsOnline`, `Online Book` |
| …and more | Case-insensitive & whitespace-insensitive matching throughout |

traceEvents.toolResult: {"type":"plan","query_summary":"Update the Services Data Extracter Agent and its skill to support intelligent CSV column mapping — fuzzy/alias matching of uploaded CSV headers to target schema fields.","steps":[{"step":1,"name":"Load agent context","description":"Retrieve agent editor context and skill instructions","status":"done"},{"step":2,"name":"Design intelligent column mapping logic","description":"Define aliases and fuzzy-matching rules for all target fields so any CSV column variation is correctly mapped","status":"in-progress"},{"step":3,"name":"Update SKILL.md","description":"Rewrite partoplay-services-importer/SKILL.md to include the column mapping intelligence step","status":"todo"},{"step":4,"name":"Update agent instructions","description":"Add the column mapping intelligence section to the agent's main instructions","status":"todo"},{"step":5,"name":"Present changes for confirmation","description":"Show full summary of all pending changes to the user and wait for approval","status":"todo"},{"step":6,"name":"Save agent","description":"Call save_agent after user confirmation","status":"todo"}]}
